step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture shows an automated, single-tenant deployment stack for financial risk reporting. On the left, we have the deployment orchestrator represented by ECS and AMI (with mentions of Terraform, Jenkins, and Ansible). The bottom section contains the core Application Stack with RDS (OLTP database) and Redshift (OLAP data warehouse), which connects to S3 via Redshift Spectrum for warm/historical storage. The top-right shows the security, compliance, and governance block consisting of KMS, Security Hub, GuardDuty, AWS Config, CloudWatch, and CloudTrail, which secure and monitor the application environment and store audit/log data in S3.

graph.name: AxiomSL: Risk and Regulatory Reporting Platform on AWS
graph.notes: Automated single-tenant SaaS architecture deploying isolated application stacks using ECS, AMI, RDS, and Redshift. Uses Redshift Spectrum to query historical data in S3 and native AWS services for security compliance.

nodes.name: ECS Orchestrator
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Handles automated container orchestration as part of the application stack.

nodes.name: CIS-Hardened AMIs
nodes.notes: CIS-hardened base machine images used for standardizing deployments.

nodes.name: RDS (OLTP)
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Handles transactional relational database workloads.

nodes.name: Redshift (OLAP)
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Used for complex regulatory reporting queries. Leverages RA3 nodes to scale compute and storage independently.

nodes.name: Amazon S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Acts as warm historical storage and secure data lake.

nodes.name: KMS Key Management
nodes.notes: Provides key storage and encryption at rest/transit.

nodes.name: Security Hub
nodes.notes: Aggregates and prioritizes security alerts and compliance checks.

nodes.name: GuardDuty
nodes.notes: Intelligent threat detection monitoring AWS accounts and workloads.

nodes.name: AWS Config
nodes.notes: Tracks resource configuration changes and simplifies compliance auditing.

nodes.name: CloudWatch
nodes.notes: Monitors resources and applications, generating operational metrics.

nodes.name: CloudTrail
nodes.notes: Records and audits all user activity and API calls across the stack.

edges.notes: Redshift Spectrum queries warm historical data stored in S3.
Application resources leverage KMS for data encryption.
Redshift clusters use KMS keys for encryption.
Audit and operational logs stored securely in S3.
